title: Tart Cherried Venezuelan Cherries Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful dish from Venezuela, combining the sweetness of cherries with the savory flavors of a tagine.
instructions: Rinse and dry the cherries, then chop them coarsely.
In a small pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and ginger, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth, heavy cream, honey, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the chopped cherries and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until they are heated through.
To assemble the tagine, place a portion of the cherry mixture in the bottom of each ceramic tagine dish.
Sprinkle the almonds, pistachios, shredded coconut, and sesame seeds over the top of each tagine.
Drizzle the remaining cherry sauce over the top of each tagine.
Cover the tagine with a lid and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the top is golden brown.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.
